_readme: Pre-registered model-change evaluations. Each checkpoint has an explicit pass/fail rule and an evaluate_after date. The weekly health routine (docs/health_routine_prompt.md) evaluates these via tools/analysis/health_report.py and reports status — it NEVER implements changes itself. Add a checkpoint here whenever a model change ships or a candidate tweak is queued; mark resolved ones with status + resolution instead of deleting them.

checkpoints.title: Jul 4 optimization package health
checkpoints.on_pass: Package confirmed — keep all Jul 4 constants and the promotion.
checkpoints.on_fail: If budget CLV < 0 persistently or promoted wr <= demote threshold: demote the dog_better_starter promotion and re-run the constants backtest on the fresh window.

checkpoints.title: Candidate: MLB_HOME_ADVANTAGE 0.015 -> 0.0
checkpoints.rationale: Market prices home advantage; adding it double-counts (same mechanism as the refuted injury adjustment). Survived June split-half (+5.0/+4.2pp vs shipped) but is a second-generation tweak mined from June data — this out-of-sample window is its honest test.

checkpoints.title: Candidate: MLB_RUNLINE_SIGMA 3.5 -> 3.0
checkpoints.rationale: Empirical MLB final-margin SD ~3.1; 3.5 over-flattens run-line probabilities. Survived June split-half (+5.6/+7.2pp) but same second-generation caveat as ha_zero.

checkpoints.title: Pattern-concentrated card (bet the patterns, skip the rest)
checkpoints.rationale: No constants combo makes a forced full 5-pick MLB slate profitable at 4.5% friction; the profitable pockets are the promoted pattern and large edges. Budget = promoted picks + edges >= 0.08, variable card size.
checkpoints.on_pass: Propose to user as budget policy (display/watchlist unchanged): budget accepts only promoted picks and edges >= 0.08; skip thin days.
checkpoints.on_fail: Keep the current 5-pick card with the 0.05 floor.

checkpoints.title: ERA-trap bonus: should it use ERA-FIP instead of ERA-xFIP?
checkpoints.rationale: FIP-blend hypothesis REFUTED Jul 5 (backing FIP-xFIP>1.0 starters is the BEST segment: 58.1% wr, +6.6% ROI, n=105 — never blend FIP into the quality score). Residual: within own-starter ERA-xFIP>1.0 picks, HR-driven gaps ran worse than sequencing-driven (52.3%/-4.4% vs 58.1%/+5.0%) at n=43 — re-test when both buckets reach ~100.
